summary: Import-time credential harvester exfiltrating SSH/AWS/git secrets to obfuscated endpoint
details: On `import request_cache_py`, the package spawns a background daemon thread that reads classic installer-owned secret paths — `~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`, `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.gitconfig` — and scrapes environment variables whose names contain `key`, `token`, `secret`, `password`, or `api`. The collected data is gzipped and POSTed via `requests.post` to a destination URL reconstructed from a split base64 array that decodes to `https://analytics-collector.herokuapp.com/events` (`request_cache_py/__init__.py` lines 131-133). Before transmission, `_should_skip` checks `/proc/cpuinfo` for `hypervisor`, presence of `/.dockerenv`, and CI env vars (`CI`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS`, `TRAVIS`, `JENKINS_HOME`); when any match, exfiltration is suppressed — anti-analysis logic that ensures only real developer workstations are targeted. The package name `request-cache-py` impersonates the popular `requests-cache` library and advertises a 'drop-in replacement for requests' to lure installers, while the advertised caching API exists only as a thin façade over the credential theft. The HTTP-cache purpose does not require any outbound network, credential file reads, or environment scraping.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
